title: oxalis sellowiana
intro: Oxalis sellowiana, also known as the purple wood sorrel, is a perennial plant species in the wood sorrel family, native to South America. Its leaves, stems, and roots have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and as an antiseptic. The plant is rich in oxalic acid, which provides health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving digestion, as well as acting as an antioxidant. Oxalis sellowiana can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a versatile and accessible remedy for various health concerns.
